title: limonium acutifolium subsp. cornusianum
intro: Limonium acutifolium subsp. cornusianum, commonly known as Sea Lavender, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Sea Lavender is often used as a dried flower, essential oil, or infusion, and is commonly used in teas, tinctures, and aromatherapy products.
